props.caption: The West builds the machinery to finance, authorize and field autonomy at scale

items.note: Ukraine hit the St. Petersburg oil terminal about 1,100 km from its border and claimed strikes on the Kronstadt naval base, part of Zelensky's 'long-range sanctions' campaign. Russia acknowledged the terminal hit with no casualties but did not confirm Kronstadt; its defence ministry claimed it downed 389 drones overnight.

China launched a coast-guard patrol east of Taiwan, off Hualien on the Pacific side of the island rather than in the strait, calling it law-enforcement. Taiwan's Mainland Affairs Council said Beijing has no authority in those waters, where its coast guard reported two Chinese ships about 54 nautical miles out.

Brussels proposed five cross-border defence projects and opened its first programme to take direct equity in defence firms, while a Pentagon memo consolidated authority over unmanned systems and Germany's Quantum Systems closed a $1.2bn round — the machinery to finance and field autonomy at scale.

The UN human-rights chief warned of 'another human rights catastrophe' around El Obeid in North Kordofan, citing summary executions, abductions and repeated drone strikes on the road-and-rail junction between central Sudan and Darfur.

items.what: Myanmar's Min Aung Hlaing made what his government called his first state visit to an ASEAN member since taking power, received with a guard of honour in Vientiane — the clearest break yet from the bloc's diplomatic quarantine.
Brazil's Supreme Court kept Jair Bolsonaro under house arrest for health reasons rather than returning him to a prison unit, Justice Alexandre de Moraes ruled.
The DRC began the PARTNERS treatment trial against its Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak, even as WHO said a treatment centre in Ituri was attacked and set on fire, killing two.
Figure said its Figure 03 robot began a logistics workflow at BMW's Spartanburg plant, manipulating parts while pulling a heavy cart — a harder task than its earlier pick-and-place role.
Venezuela's official death toll from the twin earthquakes rose to 2,645, with more than 12,000 injured and about 15,000 left homeless, centred on the La Guaira coast north of Caracas.
Russia's defence ministry claimed the capture of Kostiantynivka in eastern Ukraine; Kyiv denied it and said fighting on the axis continued, leaving control contested.
